response.body: {"id":"gen-1773511219-q6IloDEdrjuC7KhQHqzK","object":"chat.completion","created":1773511219,"model":"openai/gpt-oss-120b","provider":"DeepInfra","system_fingerprint":null,"choices":[{"index":0,"logprobs":null,"finish_reason":"stop","native_finish_reason":"stop","message":{"role":"assistant","content":"**OpenRouter – Core Principles (Summarized)**  \n\n1. **Open Access & Interoperability**  \n   - Provide a single, unified API that aggregates many LLM providers, making it easy for developers to switch between models without changing their code.  \n   - Encourage a “plug‑and‑play” ecosystem where new models can be added by anyone, fostering competition and innovation.\n\n2. **Transparency & Informed Choice**  \n   - Every model is listed with clear metadata: provider, licensing, pricing, capabilities, and known safety or bias considerations.  \n   - Users can see exact model versions, token limits, and any usage restrictions before they make a request.\n\n3. **Ethical Use & Safety‑First Design**  \n   - Enforce community‑driven content policies (e.g., no hate‑speech, disallowed illegal content).  \n   - Offer built‑in safety filters and configurable “moderation levels” so developers can tailor risk tolerance for their applications.  \n   - Provide easy ways to opt‑out of risky models or request higher‑safety variants.\n\n4. **Fair Pricing & Revenue Sharing**  \n   - Use a transparent, usage‑based pricing model that mirrors the underlying provider’s costs plus a modest platform fee.  \n   - Allow model owners to set custom pricing and receive royalties directly through the platform, promoting sustainable open‑source and commercial ecosystems.\n\n5. **User Privacy & Data Ownership**  \n   - No logs are stored longer than necessary for billing and debugging unless the developer explicitly opts‑in to data collection.  \n   - Users retain full ownership of the prompts, outputs, and any derived data; OpenRouter does not claim rights over them.\n\n6. **Community Governance & Open Source Roots**  \n   - Decisions about platform changes, policy updates, and new features are driven by community feedback (GitHub issues, public roadmaps, voting mechanisms).  \n   - Core components (routing logic, SDKs, docs) are open‑sourced, allowing anyone to audit, fork, or contribute.\n\n7. **Reliability & Performance Guarantees**  \n   - Provide SLA‑style uptime targets, auto‑retry mechanisms, and graceful degradation (fallback to alternative models) when a particular provider experiences downtime.  \n   - Offer standardized latency metrics and rate‑limit handling so developers can build robust, production‑grade apps.\n\n8. **Developer‑Centric Tooling**  \n   - Rich SDKs (Python, Node, Rust, etc.) and an intuitive Playground for rapid prototyping.  \n   - Built‑in analytics dashboard: track token usage, cost, response times, and safety‑filter hits per model.\n\n9. **Encouraging Responsible Innovation**  \n   - Promote research and experimentation by offering “sandbox” tiers with lower cost but **explicit** warnings about reduced safety checks.  \n   - Highlight best‑practice guides for prompt engineering, bias mitigation, and model evaluation.\n\n10. **Long‑Term Sustainability**  \n    - Support a mixed ecosystem of commercial, open‑source, and community‑hosted models, ensuring that no single vendor can dominate the market.  \n    - Regularly audit and update the platform to keep pace with evolving AI regulations and standards (e.g., GDPR, ISO‑27001).\n\n---\n\n### TL;DR\n\nOpenRouter’s principles revolve around **open, interoperable access to many LLMs**, **transparent model information**, **strong safety and privacy guarantees**, **fair pricing and revenue sharing**, **community‑driven governance**, and **developer‑friendly tooling**—all aimed at creating a sustainable, responsible, and competitive AI model marketplace.","refusal":null,"reasoning":"The user wants a summary of key points of a page: https://openrouter.ai/docs/overview/principles.
